reasoning: The informal statement says "a group of even order contains an element of order 2". The Lean statement encodes this as: for any finite group G (with `[Fintype G]`) such that `Even (card G)`, there exists x with `orderOf x = 2`. The Fintype hypothesis is implicit in the informal phrase "even order" — speaking of a group's order presupposes finiteness. `Even (card G)` correctly captures "even order", and `∃ x : G, orderOf x = 2` correctly captures "contains an element of order 2". The quantifier structure (universal over G, existential over x) matches. This is the standard Cauchy-for-p=2 formulation and is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
